sections.passage_or_context:
Lecture notes
Topic: Great Pacific Garbage Patch
General info
• A swirl of marine litter {{BLANK}}
• Located in the North Pacific Ocean
• Large area, not sure of exact size
Description
• Made of plastics, chemical {{BLANK}} other litter
• Low density, hard to see
• Held together by {{BLANK}} and movement of the water
History
• First described in {{BLANK}}
• Measured plastic in the North Pacific using estimates from Sea of Japan
• Named by Curtis {{BLANK}}

questions.content: The Great Pacific Garbage Patch is formed by
The scientific information about where ocean plastics come from
How much of the ocean litter consists of fishing gear?
What is the most surprising thing about the Great Pacific Garbage Patch?
options: B how easy it is to clean up
C how there is one identifiable source

questions.content: Why do the size estimates of the Garbage Patch vary so much?
options: A the media exaggerates the problem
B concentration standards are too high
C sampling is a complicated process
